course_title: Sport & Physical Activity Management: Industry Placement

learning_outcomes.description: Identify and appraise the structures and governance of Australian nutrition, sport and/or physical activity industries
Analyse and apply management skills and theory necessary to working within the Australian nutrition, sport and/or physical activity industry
Build professional practices and relationships, and independently engage with stakeholders
Utilise theoretical frameworks to promote healthy living to organisations and the broader community
Critique organisational decisions within an ethical decision-making framework
Identify and appraise the issues facing contemporary nutrition, sport and/or physical activity organisations within an analytic framework
Examine the end-to-end customer service journey utilising a human-centred design framework
Reflect on experiences to engage in continuing professional development
Work effectively within a team in order to develop communication and process management skills, and facilitate peer-assisted, collaborative learning

assessments.weighting: <dd>60% Part 1 (15%), Part 2 (15%), Part 3 (5% each week – total 30%) ( </dd>

assessments.task_description:
Part 1

During the week 1 workshop each student will complete a Draft Career Action Planning Worksheet to be submitted (Individual). Note: This worksheet will help each student to complete Assessment C Part 1 later in the semester. 

Part 2

Using the Draft Career Action Planning Worksheet, each student will create a Resume + Cover Letter to assist them in gaining their placement. Note: The Resume + Cover Letter are to be submitted regardless of whether the student has secured a placement or not.

Parts 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 3.6

Students will work individually and in groups to create six (6) workshop artifacts across the semester. Note: anonymous peer evaluation of team members is available via Buddycheck. Collated responses may facilitate moderation of results.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

These tasks have been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are expected to undertake all assessable workshop activities at the scheduled dates and times. Submission of Parts 1 and 2 is via Turnitin. Submission of Parts 3.1 to 3.6 is at the end of each workshop.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
A formal translation of your placement STAR experiences into an evidence‑based story that showcases your academic and professional competence, impact, and growth, including supporting evidence (e.g., literature, photos, drawings, supervisor notes)

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. Therefore, to pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of literature independent of AI and MT tools.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be submitted via the relevant Turnitin submission portal on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Part 1: Participate in and present a single page (A1 Size) visual poster at HMNS End-of-Semester Showcase. 28/10/2026 4:00 pm

Part 2: Finalised Career Action Plan linking placement insights to future employment goals. 29/07/2026 5:00 pm

Part 3: Placement Supervisor Report (note: your supervisor submits this, but you must ensure they have) 13/11/2026 5:00 pm
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to complete their 50-hour Course Placement before 5:00 pm, Friday, October 24, 2025 (Week 13). With no Workshops held in Weeks 10, 11 and 12, students have ample time to complete this assessment requirement. All course assessments relate to this placement. Placement supervisors cannot complete the confidential supervisor report, which is the assessable item for this placement, until these hours are completed. Students must note that this placement is a Pass/Fail Hurdle for this course.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
